model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Purdue holds a meaningful edge in overall adjusted efficiency — KenPom #8 (AdjEM +30.5) and Torvik #8 versus Gonzaga's KenPom #11 (AdjEM +28.16) and Torvik #11 — anchored by the nation's #1 adjusted offense (AdjOE 132.6) that is the most dangerous weapon in this matchup and runs into a Gonzaga defense operating without star forward Braden Huff, who has been out since January 15 with a dislocated kneecap and whose Sweet 16 return remains uncertain. Gonzaga's elite 9th-ranked defense is a genuine equalizer and the Bulldogs proved capable of winning without Huff during their 30-3 regular season, but Purdue's historically prolific offense — led by a fully healthy Braden Smith, Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn — presents the toughest offensive assignment Gonzaga's defense has faced all tournament. Gonzaga's own offense ranked only 30th-37th nationally faces a Purdue defense ranked 36th, offering no significant scoring advantage to offset the efficiency gap, and the Boilermakers' Big Ten battle-testing and peak form as conference tournament champions make them the right side in this Sweet 16 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs. Purdue Sweet 16 — West Region Efficiency and Injury Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue holds a ~2.3-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Gonzaga (KenPom +30.5 vs. +28.16; Torvik #8 vs. #11), driven by the nation's #1 offense against a Gonzaga defense ranked 9th nationally but operating without Braden Huff (out since Jan. 15, dislocated kneecap, Sweet 16 return uncertain). Purdue's Braden Smith, Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn are all healthy after a dominant Big Ten Tournament championship run. Gonzaga's elite defense is the key equalizer, but their offense ranked 30th-37th nationally offers no scoring advantage against Purdue's 36th-ranked defense. The net efficiency gap, Purdue's offensive dominance, and Huff's continued absence tip this to the Boilermakers at moderate confidence.
evidence: Purdue: KenPom #8, AdjEM +30.5, AdjOE 131-132.6 (#1-2 nationally), AdjDE 100.2-100.5 (#36 Torvik). Gonzaga: KenPom #11, AdjEM +28.16, AdjOE 120.3-122.1 (#30-37 nationally), AdjDE 93.9 (#9 nationally).
Braden Huff (Gonzaga) has been out since January 15 with a dislocated kneecap; Sweet 16 return described as 'on the table' but uncertain per multiple reports including Clutchpoints and Yahoo Sports.
Purdue beat Queens (R64) and Miami FL (R32) with Braden Smith (Big Ten Tournament MVP), Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn all healthy and executing at an elite level.
Gonzaga compiled their 30-3 record largely without Huff, demonstrating depth, but the Sweet 16 opponent quality (Purdue's #1 offense) is the toughest test that depth has faced.
West Region bracket analysis (SI.com, Covers.com) highlighted Purdue's record-setting offense as a primary strength and key factor in their Sweet 16 matchup against Gonzaga.

The NCAA Tournament contest tips off at 7:39 PM ET. The Boilermakers enter their matchup against the Bulldogs as a 4.5-point favorite. The matchup features an over/under of 155.5. Continue reading to get everything you need to know before placing bets on the Purdue-Gonzaga clash. Purdue vs. Gonzaga Game Information & Odds When: Friday, March 29, 2024 at 7:39 PM ET Where: Little Caesars Arena in Detroit, Michigan How to Watch on TV: TBS Live boxscore on FOX Sports Boilermakers vs Bulldogs Betting Information Favorite Spread Favorite Spread Odds Underdog Spread Odds Total Over Total Odds Under Total Odds Favorite Moneyline Underdog Moneyline Boilermakers -4.5 -108 -112 155.5 -112 -108 -198 +164 Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction Pick ATS: Gonzaga (+4.5) Pick OU: Under (155.5) Prediction: Purdue 78, Gonzaga 75 Learn more about the Purdue Boilermakers vs. the Gonzaga Bulldogs game on FOX Sports! Purdue vs. Gonzaga Betting Insights Betting Line Implied Predictions Based on the spread and over/under, the implied score for the matchup is Boilermakers 80, Bulldogs 76. The Boilermakers have a 66.4% chance to claim victory in this contest per the moneyline's implied probability. The Bulldogs have a 37.9% implied probability to come out on top. Key Spread Facts Purdue is 15-14-2 ATS this season. Gonzaga is 15-16-0 ATS this year. Purdue has covered the spread 12 times this season (12-14-1 ATS) when playing as at least 4.5-point favorites. Gonzaga has covered the spread once when an underdog by 4.5 points or more this season (in two opportunities). Key Total Facts Boilermakers games have finished with more than 155.5 points 12 times so far this year. Bulldogs games have gone over 155.5 points on 13 occasions this year. These teams score 168.9 points per game combined, higher than the over/under for this matchup by 13.4. Key Moneyline Facts Purdue has been the moneyline favorite 25 times this season. They've finished 21-4 in those games. This season, Gonzaga has won two out of the four games in which it has been the underdog. Purdue has a record of 19-4 when it has played as a moneyline favorite with odds of -198 or shorter (winning 82.6%). result.title: Is Braden Huff Playing in March Madness? Latest Injury Update on ...
result.snippet: Braden Huff has already missed 15 consecutive games for Gonzaga , and this could very well turn into 16 by this weekend. Out since Jan. 15, Huff reportedly hurt his knee before the game against Washington State. Initially, the injury was thought to need four or some weeks, but it has now been eight weeks, and the latest injury update isn't bright.

result.text: Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction, Odds, Picks - NCAA Tournament Sweet 16 | FOX Sports my favs Access and manage your favorites here DISMISS Scores Watch Betting WBC ⚾ FOX Super 6 💰 Stories Search Sign In Account SPORTS & TEAMS PLAYERS SHOWS PERSONALITIES TOPICS POPULAR SEARCHES - No Data Available - BROWSE BY SPORTS & TEAMS PLAYERS SHOWS PERSONALITIES TOPICS Built on Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction, Odds, Picks - NCAA Tournament Sweet 16 Updated Mar. 29, 2024 8:27 p.m. ET share facebook x reddit link Data Skrive A Sweet 16 matchup will feature the No. 1 seed Purdue Boilermakers (31-4) and the No. 5 seed Gonzaga Bulldogs (27-7) playing on Friday at Little Caesars Arena. The NCAA Tournament contest tips off at 7:39 PM ET. The Boilermakers enter their matchup against the Bulldogs as a 4.5-point favorite. The matchup features an over/under of 155.5. Continue reading to get everything you need to know before placing bets on the Purdue-Gonzaga clash. Purdue vs. Gonzaga Game Information & Odds When: Friday, March 29, 2024 at 7:39 PM ET Where: Little Caesars Arena in Detroit, Michigan How to Watch on TV: TBS Live boxscore on FOX Sports Boilermakers vs Bulldogs Betting Information Favorite Spread Favorite Spread Odds Underdog Spread Odds Total Over Total Odds Under Total Odds Favorite Moneyline Underdog Moneyline Boilermakers -4.5 -108 -112 155.5 -112 -108 -198 +164 Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction Pick ATS: Gonzaga (+4.5) Pick OU: Under (155.5) Prediction: Purdue 78, Gonzaga 75 Learn more about the Purdue Boilermakers vs. the Gonzaga Bulldogs game on FOX Sports! Purdue vs. Gonzaga Betting Insights Betting Line Implied Predictions Based on the spread and over/under, the implied score for the matchup is Boilermakers 80, Bulldogs 76. The Boilermakers have a 66.4% chance to claim victory in this contest per the moneyline's implied probability. The Bulldogs have a 37.9% implied probability to come out on top. Key Spread Facts Purdue is 15-14-2 ATS this season. Gonzaga is 15-16-0 ATS this year. Purdue has covered the spread 12 times this season (12-14-1 ATS) when playing as at least 4.5-point favorites. Gonzaga has covered the spread once when an underdog by 4.5 points or more this season (in two opportunities). Key Total Facts Boilermakers games have finished with more than 155.5 points 12 times so far this year. Bulldogs games have gone over 155.5 points on 13 occasions this year. These teams score 168.9 points per game combined, higher than the over/under for this matchup by 13.4. Key Moneyline Facts Purdue has been the moneyline favorite 25 times this season. They've finished 21-4 in those games. This season, Gonzaga has won two out of the four games in which it has been the underdog. Purdue has a record of 19-4 when it has played as a moneyline favorite with odds of -198 or shorter (winning 82.6%). Gonzaga has played as an underdog of +164 or more once this season and lost that game. Purdue vs. Gonzaga: Recent Results Boilermakers vs Bulldogs Recent Games Date F

modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"s16-west-2","winnerId":"purdue","confidence":0.62,"rationale":"Purdue holds a meaningful edge in overall adjusted efficiency — KenPom #8 (AdjEM +30.5) and Torvik #8 versus Gonzaga's KenPom #11 (AdjEM +28.16) and Torvik #11 — anchored by the nation's #1 adjusted offense (AdjOE 132.6) that is the most dangerous weapon in this matchup and runs into a Gonzaga defense operating without star forward Braden Huff, who has been out since January 15 with a dislocated kneecap and whose Sweet 16 return remains uncertain. Gonzaga's elite 9th-ranked defense is a genuine equalizer and the Bulldogs proved capable of winning without Huff during their 30-3 regular season, but Purdue's historically prolific offense — led by a fully healthy Braden Smith, Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn — presents the toughest offensive assignment Gonzaga's defense has faced all tournament. Gonzaga's own offense ranked only 30th-37th nationally faces a Purdue defense ranked 36th, offering no significant scoring advantage to offset the efficiency gap, and the Boilermakers' Big Ten battle-testing and peak form as conference tournament champions make them the right side in this Sweet 16 matchup."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"s16-west-2-analysis","title":"Gonzaga vs. Purdue Sweet 16 — West Region Efficiency and Injury Analysis","summary":"Purdue holds a ~2.3-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Gonzaga (KenPom +30.5 vs. +28.16; Torvik #8 vs. #11), driven by the nation's #1 offense against a Gonzaga defense ranked 9th nationally but operating without Braden Huff (out since Jan. 15, dislocated kneecap, Sweet 16 return uncertain). Purdue's Braden Smith, Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn are all healthy after a dominant Big Ten Tournament championship run. Gonzaga's elite defense is the key equalizer, but their offense ranked 30th-37th nationally offers no scoring advantage against Purdue's 36th-ranked defense. The net efficiency gap, Purdue's offensive dominance, and Huff's continued absence tip this to the Boilermakers at moderate confidence.","evidence":["Purdue: KenPom #8, AdjEM +30.5, AdjOE 131-132.6 (#1-2 nationally), AdjDE 100.2-100.5 (#36 Torvik). Gonzaga: KenPom #11, AdjEM +28.16, AdjOE 120.3-122.1 (#30-37 nationally), AdjDE 93.9 (#9 nationally).","Braden Huff (Gonzaga) has been out since January 15 with a dislocated kneecap; Sweet 16 return described as 'on the table' but uncertain per multiple reports including Clutchpoints and Yahoo Sports.","Purdue beat Queens (R64) and Miami FL (R32) with Braden Smith (Big Ten Tournament MVP), Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn all healthy and executing at an elite level.","Gonzaga compiled their 30-3 record largely without Huff, demonstrating depth, but the Sweet 16 opponent quality (Purdue's #1 offense) is the toughest test that depth has faced.","West Region bracket analysis (SI.com, Covers.com) highlighted Purdue's record-setting offense as a primary strength and key factor in their Sweet 16 matchup against Gonzaga."]}}
